title: weinmannia auriculata var. dryadifolia
intro: Weinmannia auriculata var. dryadifolia, also known as a type of tree endemic to the islands of Tahiti and Moorea in French Polynesia, is a plant that belongs to the Cunoniaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional Polynesian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Weinmannia auriculata var. dryadifolia include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as potential uses in treating various skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its native and endangered status.
